Here is the latest in world news.

• Pakistan launched airstrikes near the Afghan border
• Casualties are reported after the cross-border military strikes HEADLINNE.com #Geopolitics #TechNews

Today's top tech stories in a nutshell.
• China has built the world's fastest supercomputer again
• Prosecutors used ChatGPT logs as evidence in an arson trial
• Ford is bringing back veteran engineers after AI work fell short HEADLINNE.com #TechNews #Headlinne

linkedin.title: I started building a news app when I was a teenager because I was tired of scrolling through noise.
linkedin.body:
Most news apps felt like they were designed for someone else. They were either too cluttered, too biased, or just plain overwhelming. I wanted something that felt like a conversation, not a broadcast.

Building Headlinne wasn't about trying to replace journalism. It was about solving a personal problem. I wanted a way to see the stories that actually mattered to me without having to dig through irrelevant headlines for twenty minutes every morning.

We used AI not to write the news, but to act as a filter. It helps us understand what you care about so the app can adapt to your interests. It is a tool to help you reclaim your time and stay informed on your own terms.

The goal has always been simple. We want to make the news feel personal again.
linkedin.cta: If you are curious about a better way to stay updated, you can see how it works at HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: AI chipmakers see massive stock gains
slides.explanation: Stock prices for companies building AI hardware have tripled this year. Investors are betting big on the tech powering the current AI boom.

slides.headline: Violence flares on Pakistan-Afghan border
slides.explanation: Pakistan launched airstrikes in eastern Afghanistan after claiming they were targeting militants. Dozens of casualties have been reported following the attacks.

slides.headline: Pakistan cites terror attacks as strike reason
slides.explanation: Officials in Pakistan state these recent military strikes were a direct response to attacks against innocent people. Tensions between the two nations remain high.

slides.headline: One Nation withdraws funding claims
slides.explanation: Pauline Hanson’s party pulled back over $800,000 in election spending claims. This follows questions from the Australian Electoral Commission about their funding.

slides.headline: Victoria premier defends infrastructure costs
slides.explanation: Jacinta Allan is blaming inflation rather than corruption for rising costs on major projects. She is currently resisting calls for a royal commission investigation.

instagram.caption: Stay up to date with today's top global stories in just a few minutes. From shifting markets to international conflicts and local politics, we have the facts you need. Check out our latest update to see what is happening around the world. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: China takes back supercomputer crown
slides.explanation: Despite US trade restrictions on tech parts, China now holds the top spot on the TOP500 list again. This is the first time they have led since 2018, beating out the previous record holder.

slides.headline: ChatGPT logs used in arson trial
slides.explanation: Prosecutors in a major California wildfire case used AI chat history as evidence. Along with phone data and video footage, the logs helped build the case against the defendant.

slides.headline: Ford brings back veteran engineers
slides.explanation: Ford realized that relying too heavily on AI for product quality was a mistake. They are now rehiring experienced engineers to fix the gaps left by over-automation.

slides.headline: Is convenience ruining our lives?
slides.explanation: Writer Ian Bogost argues that Silicon Valley might be focusing on the wrong things. He suggests that embracing the small, manual tasks could help us reclaim our lives from too much tech.

slides.headline: A pricey new bike lock
slides.explanation: A company that usually secures bank ATMs is launching a premium bike lock for $280. While it is expensive, the makers hope the high quality and insurance benefits will justify the cost.

instagram.caption: From AI in the courtroom to the return of classic engineering, here is your tech news roundup for today. Swipe through to catch up on the stories everyone is talking about. Stay informed with Headlinne. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
